title: Endurance: Shackleton's Incredible Voyage
author: Alfred Lansing
content: “The task was not to save the mission, but to preserve the unit.”
action: Prioritize the integrity of your core team and systems over original objectives when environments turn non-survivable.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
content: “He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.”
action: Anchor your internal purpose as a buffer against extreme systemic and psychological stress.

title: Into Thin Air
author: Jon Krakauer
content: “The higher you go, the more the rules of the game change.”
action: Recognize that baseline technical competence is insufficient for extreme, high-risk scenarios; adapt your strategy accordingly.

title: The Right Stuff
author: Tom Wolfe
content: “The right stuff... meant that you were willing to push it to the very edge.”
action: Cultivate high-variance, high-reward decision-making as a deliberate trait for navigating extreme boundaries.

title: Survival in the Killing Fields
author: Haing Ngor
content: “To keep your identity is to keep your life.”
action: Protect your internal cognitive structure and identity as the final line of defense against systemic eradication.
